feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to create a Dynamic entity and view that table in UI using SAP Fiori elements
entry.content:
I am a CAPM developer , As I'm new to this technology I need some suggestions from you.

I have a requirement of creating a table in SAP HANA Cloud Database with end user's input from the UI. Is it possible to create a table without defining the entity in schema and also view that table in the UI using SAP Fiori elements. Please help me with your suggestion.

Thanks & Regards. 
Livingston Immanual.

entry.title: SAP Build Apps Import Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am learning from https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/build-apps-cap-app.html tutorial.

I am getting error while importing RiskManagement.mtar file to https://ABCDEtrial.us10.build.cloud.sap/lobby  (My SAP BTP Trial account).

I can upload RiskManagement.gpg file to lobby provided in above tutorial without any errors.

How can I identify gap in my BAS code.

Application is developed in BAS tool and able to fetch data from 

"curl https://RiskManagement-RiskManagementService.dest/Risks" command. 

 

 

 

Thank you in Advance.

Prasanna

entry.title: Connecting Splash BI tool to S4HANA and BW4HANA
entry.content: There is a non-SAP tool known as Splash BI used for reporting. For one of the demand we need to integrate it with S/4HANA and BW/4HANA for reporting but issue is that we unable to find any connector in both .So please let us know how we can integrate SAP systems to Splash BI.

entry.title: API Hub Creation failed
entry.content:
Hello, I was following the tutorial:
https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-full-stack-applications-using-productivity-tools-in-sap-business-application-studio/consuming-an-external-service-from-sap-s-4hana_faa318cd-9b48-48c4-b5fc-e6c205fb7685

But I am not able to create the API Business Hub. That is why I am stuck at the following step: Consuming an External Service From SAP S/4HANA
How do I fix it? Is it for paid plans only?

entry.title: OData development & Registration on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi,

We currently having a centralized deployment [Hub Model] of SAP Gateway , wherein we develop and register OData services. Since we are having an objective to replace the on-premise SAP Gateway instance during the cloud migration, we would like to know whether  any BTP services are available for OData development & registration in cloud . Not sure whether OData provisioning services is meant for that?

Kindly advice.

Regards,

entry.title: SAP build Process Automation is not capturing SAP GUI window completely
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to capture SAP GUI screen through build process automation, but it is not loading complete screen.

Please suggest how we can capture screen completely.

entry.title: Is it possible to combine multiple Fiori floor plans to create a single application
entry.content:
Hi all ,
     In my case, I want to develop an application with custom Fiori UI and also List and object page as well, need to navigate between multiple paged so , I thought of combining both basic and List report floor plan of Fiori UI.
    Using List report floor plan will increase my productivity and Basic Floor plan will increase flexibility.

    Is it possible to combine those floorplans to develop a single application?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Error while creating a decisions in Build Process Automation - Again?
entry.content:
I'm trying to add a decision object to a SAP Build Process Automation project. After creating and configuring the decision I close the decision configuration screen and can see that the decision object found in the overview of objects in the project is stored with a almost transparent font.

 The decision object disappears (automatically deleted) in the overview of objects on the project.

I have raised similar question previously (Solved: Error while creating a decision - SAP Community) and the issue was solved. But it seems like the issue with decision creations in Build Process Automation occurs again. 

Has anyone done the same observations?

entry.title: 2nd Problem calling on Excel Data with Process Automation
entry.content: Something seems to have changed, and I can no longer link excel worksheets to my automation. The screenshot "current member fail.jpg" should allow me to select a value that looks like that in "value should be.jpg".

entry.title: Re: SAP build Process Automation is not capturing SAP GUI...
entry.content:
I am also trying to capture the below SAP GUI screen.

  

After screen capture it is not showing full parts of the screen. Because of it not able to access User Input field and Password Input field.

Please suggest how to resolve this issue.

Thanks,

Siddhant

entry.title: Build Process Automation and Work Zone Connection different subaccounts?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we have been using SAP Build Process Automation  for a year to automate our processes. In the future we want to use the workflow capability of BPA. That's the reason why we want to connect Build Process Automation with Build Work Zone, standard edition. Is it necessary that both applications are located in the same subaccount in the BTP or can they have different subaccounts?

Kind regards,

Theresa

entry.title: Integrating Abby Vantage to SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi all,

I am looking for sap connectors for Abby vantage to integrate with SAP CPI. Does anyone know or have any leads?

Thanks,

Prasanthi

entry.title: Implementing XSUAA for User Authentication in SAP CAP with HANA Database
entry.content:
Hello SAP Enthusiasts,

I am in the midst of architecting a SAP CAP-based backend coupled with SAP UI5 on the frontend. The objective is to weave in Authentication and Authorization functionalities, particularly utilizing XSUAA, while user credentials are securely stored in the HANA Cloud Database.

Imagine we have a login page. Upon entering their details and hitting the submit button, the system is designed to authenticate users against the records in the HANA Cloud Database. Successful authentication would result in the issuance of a token, which is then used to authorize subsequent API requests.

I would appreciate any insights or guidance on how to effectively implement this requirement.

entry.title: How to keep desktop agent session alive with unattended mode in RDP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

How to keep desktop agent session alive always with unattended mode in RDP, after few hours if desktop agent is not in use it is getting disconnected in the RDP.

Could you please help me how I can keep the desktop Agent session alive always with unattended mode.

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: SAP MDO Business Object Type Workforce Person: populate extension fields from PerPerson
entry.content:
Hey guys,
new day with MDI - new issues with MDI!

We have a working sandbox environment with SAP MDI receiving employee master data from SuccessFactors Employee Central. We're using SAP MDO Business Object Type "Workforce Person 3.5.0", which has a handful of custom fields customized under "Extension Fields". It is all working fine and the employee master data incl. the extension fields is successfully and correctly imported into the Workforce Person object until a field from SuccessFactors Employee Central personInfo (PerPerson) entity is mapped.

PROBLEM: how can fields from EC personInfo (e.g. Date of Birth/Place of Birth) be mapped to Workforce Person 3.5.0 ProfileDetails?
Example: I have added "Place of Birth" (personInfo-->placeOfBirth) as an extension field under ProfileDetails (sap.odm.workforce.ProfileDetails) {image001.png}.

Once the field was activated, I added the required picklist item with external Code "ProfileDetails_ext__personInfo_pob" to picklist BCUI_MAPPED_DOMAIN_ATTRIBUTE_NAME, to make the target field available for selection under "Manage Business Configuration" --> "personInfo" {image002.png}.

Then I went to "Manage Business Configuration" --> "personInfo" --> Details for "Place of Birth", and selected the previously created target field name "ProfileDetails_ext__personInfo_pob" under "Mapped Domain Attribute Name" {image003.png}.

My expectation is, that the extraction Job then derives the target field in ODM based on the picklist item selected under "Mapped Domain Attribute Name". So the field value for Place of Birth should be mapped to "ProfileDetails" --> "ext__personInfo_pob".

What happens is, the extraction report is failing with message: "ProfileDetails_ext__personInfo_pob in the path is invalid" {image004.png}.

Checking the payload of the failed job execution, it indeed looks a bit weird {image005.png}, because the extension field for Place of Birth is not listed under the ProfileDetails section like the other extension fields (e.g. gender, nationality) but creates an own section.

It almost seems to me, that the setup itself in SAP MDI/MDO and EC are 100% correct but somehow the Integration Center Report behind the Business Scenario is extracting the queried data into a wrong structure in the Payload, which is then failing when handed over to the MDI API.

Questions:

  • Does anyone have an explanation for this behavior?
  • Does anyone ever successfully map a personInfo field to an extension field under ProfileDetails?
  • Any known workarounds for this?

Thanks for any kind of advice, pointing me into the right direction!
Cheers!

entry.title: CAP vs. Spring Boot
entry.content: Whats the benefit of using SAP CAP instead of Java Spring Boot with SAP Cloud SDK especially if we use Angular as front end  instead of UI5

entry.title: IDOCs (INVOIC02) basic type
entry.content:
I have copied INVOIC02 and created ZINVOIC02. My question is: Are all user exits from INVOIC02 also automatically copied into ZINVOIC02 new custom type? 
OR do I need to make developments/implementations in order for that to work?

entry.title: From Idea to Market: Accelerating MVP Development with Rapid Prototyping
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced business landscape, getting your product to market quickly and efficiently can mean the difference between success and failure. This is where the concept of rapid prototyping shines, especially in the realm of Minimum Viable Product (MVP) development.

In this article, we'll delve into how rapid prototyping can empower the MVP Development Company to transform ideas into market-ready MVPs quickly and efficiently.

Understanding Rapid Prototyping

Rapid prototyping is a development approach that emphasizes building quick, scaled-down versions of a product to test its functionality, design, and market viability. Unlike traditional methods that involve extensive planning upfront, rapid prototyping focuses on getting a tangible product in front of users as soon as possible.

The Rapid Prototyping Process

Rapid prototyping is a product development method to swiftly create an initial version of a product or its part for validating the design. Here's an overview of the rapid prototyping process:

  • Idea Generation: The process starts with brainstorming and idea generation sessions to outline the basic concept of the product.
  • Conceptualization: Once the idea is formed, it's time to create rough sketches, wireframes, or simple mockups to visualize the product.
  • Prototyping: This stage involves building a basic prototype of the product. It can range from low-fidelity paper prototypes to high-fidelity interactive prototypes.
  • Testing and Feedback: The prototype is then tested with real users to gather feedback on its usability, features, and overall concept.
  • Iteration: Based on the feedback received, iterations are made to the prototype to improve its functionality and user experience.

Advantages of Rapid Prototyping in MVP Development

Rapid prototyping plays a crucial role in MVP (Minimum Viable Product) development by allowing teams to quickly validate ideas, gather feedback, and iterate on product concepts. Here are some advantages of using rapid prototyping in MVP development:

  • Speed: Rapid prototyping significantly reduces the time it takes to go from an idea to a functional prototype, enabling faster market entry.
  • Cost-effectiveness: By identifying and addressing issues early in the development process, rapid prototyping saves both time and resources.
  • User-Centric Design: Early user feedback allows for the development of products that better meet user needs and preferences.
  • Flexibility: Rapid prototyping allows for quick iterations and changes, ensuring the final product is aligned with market demands.

Successfully MVPs Developed with Rapid Prototyping

Rapid prototyping is a fantastic approach for developing MVPs (Minimum Viable Products) quickly and efficiently. Here are several successful MVPs developed using rapid prototyping:

  1. Snapchat: Snapchat amously started as a simple prototype called Picaboo. Through rapid prototyping and user feedback, it evolved into the popular social media platform we know today.
  2. Dropbox: Dropbox created a basic video demo to showcase their idea before writing a single line of code. This approach helped them validate the concept and attract early adopters.
  3. Zappos: Zappos initially tested the concept of online shoe retailing with a simple website and photos taken at local shoe stores. This rapid prototyping approach validated their business idea before investing heavily in inventory.

Tools and Techniques for Rapid Prototyping

Rapid prototyping is crucial for quickly validating ideas, iterating on designs, and getting early feedback from users before investing significant time and resources. Here are various tools and techniques for rapid prototyping:

  • Wireframing Tools: Tools like Balsamiq, Sketch, or Figma are popular for creating low-fidelity wireframes.
  • Prototyping Platforms: Platforms such as InVision, Adobe XD, or Proto.io allow for creating interactive prototypes.
  • MVP Frameworks: Using frameworks like Lean Startup or Design Sprint can streamline the rapid prototyping process.

Conclusion

Rapid prototyping is a powerful methodology for accelerating MVP development, allowing companies to iterate quickly, reduce time-to-market, and build products that resonate with users. By embracing rapid prototyping, startups and businesses can increase their chances of success in today's competitive market.

entry.title: SAP IAS User group patch action is not working from Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are using the Identity directory API for the Patch action of the group. I have created the user using IAS action, wanted to move the user to specific Group. From Business accelerator Hub I am able to move the user to desired group, but from action group it is giving me error 400.

I am following the below steps:

  1. Login to SAP Build Process lobby, navigate to action section.
  2. Add the Identity provider API Action Project.
  3. Add the Group Patch Action to the project
  4. And execute the Patch action with provided input as Group Id 
  5. And operation as "xxxxx", path as "xxxxx" and value as "xxxxx"

    Attached screenshot for your reference:

     

    ERROR: 

Could you please help me or any suggestion to resolve this issue?

Thanks,

Rupesh

entry.title: How can the manifest be dynamically provided in Component.js?
entry.content:
Is it possible to modify manifest or provide a new manifest dynamically in component.js?
Any example or reference is available?

Or any hooks available to change manifest dyanmically?

Note: Want to change routes pattern and targets.
